items.name: Job.Info
items.protection: unverified — protection claimed by comment but callers not visible in this file
items.comment_claim: Line 14 comment states: 'ID and Owner are set once at init; Status and ExitCode are mutated (JM's mutex)'
items.status_reason: The struct field at line 15 is accessed as a whole value, but enforcement of the claimed JobManager RWMutex is not visible inside job.go itself; must be verified in manager.go call sites.

items.name: Job.Cmd
items.owner: set once at init, then read-only
items.protection: immutable after init per comment
items.comment_claim: Line 16 comment states: 'set once at init and never written again'
items.status_reason: The comment at line 16 claims write-once semantics, but this file alone cannot confirm no other goroutine writes Cmd after construction; callers in manager.go must be checked.

items.status_reason: Field declared at line 20 with a comment claiming JobManager RWMutex protection, but no lock acquisition is visible in job.go; all read/write sites must be audited in manager.go.

items.name: Job.output

items.status_reason: Field declared at line 23; the pointer itself may be set once at init, but the comment implies mutation is protected by a Cond mutex that lives inside OutputBuffer — this cannot be verified from job.go alone.

items.name: JobManager.mu
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: JobManager.jobs
items.protection: sync.RWMutex (jm.mu): writes under Lock(), reads under RLock()
items.comment_claim: Top-level struct comment states 'using mutex to protect the job lifecycles state'
items.status_reason: All four access sites are demonstrably covered: write at line 57 under mu.Lock()/Unlock() (lines 55–58); read in Status() at line 77 under mu.RLock()/defer RUnlock() (lines 75–76); read in Stop() at line ~108 under mu.Lock() (line ~105); read in Output() at line ~155 under mu.RLock()/RUnlock(); no access site is found outside a held lock.

items.name: OutputBuffer.mu
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: OutputBuffer.cond
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: OutputBuffer.buf
items.owner: OutputBuffer — written by Write(), read by Read() via OutputBufferReader
items.protection: ob.mu (sync.Mutex) held at every access site
items.comment_claim: Write() doc says it 'appends chunk to buffer'; no explicit claim about which lock protects buf, but the mutex acquisition is structurally adjacent
items.status_reason: Written at line 38 under ob.mu.Lock() (line 36); read at lines 57 and 61 inside Read() which holds obr.ob.mu.Lock() at line 48 — same mutex object in all cases.

items.name: OutputBuffer.done
items.owner: OutputBuffer — written by OutputBuffer.Close(), read by OutputBufferReader.Read()
items.protection: ob.mu (sync.Mutex) held at every access site
items.comment_claim: OutputBuffer.Close() comment claims 'marks buffer done and wakes readers', implying done is the authoritative signal; no explicit claim about which lock guards it
items.status_reason: Written at line 85 under ob.mu.Lock() (line 82); read at lines 57 and 68 inside Read() which holds obr.ob.mu.Lock() at line 48 — same mutex object.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.ob
items.owner: OutputBufferReader — set once at construction, never reassigned
items.protection: immutable after construction; no lock required for the pointer itself
items.comment_claim: none
items.status_reason: Assigned once at line 30 (NewReader, single-goroutine init) and thereafter only used to reach ob's fields which are individually protected by ob.mu; the pointer value itself is never mutated.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.offset
items.protection: ob.mu (sync.Mutex) — offset is mutated inside Read() which holds obr.ob.mu.Lock()
items.comment_claim: none
items.status_reason: Read at line 57 and 61, written at line 62 — all within Read() which acquires obr.ob.mu.Lock() at line 48 before any of these accesses.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.closed
items.owner: OutputBufferReader — written by OutputBufferReader.Close(), read by Read()
items.protection: ob.mu (sync.Mutex) held at every access site
items.comment_claim: OutputBufferReader.Close() comment says it 'unblocks any pending Read, which returns io.ErrClosedPipe'; no explicit lock claim
items.status_reason: Written at line 95 under obr.ob.mu.Lock() (line 92); read at line 57 inside Read() which holds obr.ob.mu.Lock() at line 48 — same mutex object.
